subject: LU-6155 osd-zfs: dbuf_hold_impl() called without the lock

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

(2 comments)

I'm all for dropping this code as you've done in favor of a worst case estimate.  I've just posted some comments on how we could make a better estimate.  It would also be interesting to see how this impacts performance.

Patch Set 7: Code-Review-1

This does break sanity-quota/23.  This patch causes writes to fail that should succeed because the space estimation assumes worst case, even for already allocated data.

Patch Set 7: -Code-Review

hmm, there is no "allocated already" semantics for ZFS? every time we have to write to a new place while the already allocated blocks aren't always a subject to release (due to snapshots) .

Patch Set 9: Code-Review-1

Breaks sanity-quota/23

Patch Set 9:

Nathaniel, I'd agree with Alex that there isn't a guarantee with ZFS to be able to overwrite an existing file in-place when out of space or out of quota, because of COW nature of ZFS.  The old file contents may not be released for some time due to TXG commits or snapshots.

If this is what sanity-quota test_23 is testing, maybe that test should just be skipped for ZFS?

Patch Set 9:

(7 comments)

7 code warning(s).
If you believe the warnings are incorrect, please leave a comment with your explanations as a reply to the warning.

Patch Set 9:

PPC64 Builder: build succesful

Patch Set 9:

Andreas, yes, s-q test_23 failed on rewrite when out of quota.

Patch Set 9:

Clearly the basic assumption for the re-write portion of s-q 23, that no additional disk space is occupied, is invalid for any COW backend.

However it seems that we ought to at least be able to place an upper bound on that space required.  The quota value used in the test could be adjusted up when the backend is ZFS, to reflect this upper bound.  Then we still verify quotas are working properly in both backends.
Uploaded patch set 10.

parents.subject: LU-6852 ldlm: Do not evict MDS-MDS connection

commit.subject: LU-6155 osd-zfs: dbuf_hold_impl() called without the lock
commit.message:
LU-6155 osd-zfs: dbuf_hold_impl() called without the lock

The osd-zfs osd_count_not_mapped() calls dbuf_hold_impl() without
the required lock. In addition, dbuf_hold_impl() is an internal
function and has the expensive side effect of reading the block
from disk which would convert a full-block write into a
read-modify-write.

Since space estimation with ZFS is complicated any way, just use
the worst case as a rough estimate where a snapshot holds all current
blocks, i.e. no old space can be freed after the COW.

Skip test sanity-quota/23 on ZFS because overwrites on ZFS are not
guarenteed to be space neutral, and new worst-case assumptions will
always cause this test to fail.

Change-Id: Idf6f2ff80ff185ca8c0f38e1002ff90e457c3ca0
Signed-off-by: Isaac Huang <he.huang@intel.com>
Signed-off-by: Nathaniel Clark <nathaniel.l.clark@intel.com>
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/13541
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Alex Zhuravlev <alexey.zhuravlev@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
